title: format verb + CST + idempotence law
manager: The `format` verb — a canonical pretty-printer for FSL backed by a concrete syntax tree that retains trivia, with an executable idempotence law (formatting twice equals formatting once) that the vector corpus enforces. This is what makes FSL diffs clean and reviews sane, and the CST is what enables true byte-level round-tripping — the editor's Format button is built on exactly this, guarded by the semantic round-trip check so it can never silently corrupt a machine.
cs: `format` verb + CST/trivia retention (#134 byte-level reversibility); idempotence as an executable law in the vector corpus; fsl#134 / jssm#792 twins; the editor Format button rides this.
